data.title: Sidus Space outlines late 2026 LizzieSat-4 and LizzieSat-5 launches while expanding all-domain product commercialization
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sidus Space (SIDU) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Carol Craig stated that Sidus Space continued expanding its vertical integration, advanced the LizzieSat constellation, and strengthened partnerships serving commercial and defense customers. She explained the company aims to deliver "full-spectrum solutions from design and manufacturing to on-orbit operations and data services with the agility to meet evolving mission requirements."
  • Management highlighted a strategic shift toward diversified, recurring revenue models and detailed advancements in LizzieSat, describing the satellites as "precision-engineered microsatellites built to deliver mission-critical performance."
  • Craig announced significant progress on the mobile launch 2 contract, noting it expanded from an original value of approximately $4 million to over $8 million and is nearing completion, setting the stage for "improved gross margins and stronger revenue visibility as well as a reconfigured facility ready for expanded satellite and defense manufacturing."
  • The CEO emphasized the launch cadence, reporting that "in just over a year, we launched 3 Sidus-designed, Sidus-built hybrid 3D-printed satellites with onboard AI and multiple sensors at a pace that allowed us to rapidly integrate lessons learned into each successive mission."
  • Craig stated, "This quarter, we completed 2 successful capital raises with funds to be invested in commercializing all-domain product lines, expanding the LizzieSat constellation with LizzieSat-4 and LizzieSat-5, and advancing our Orlaith AI ecosystem."
  • Adarsh Parekh, CFO, said, "Momentum from 2024 in the first half of 2025 carried into the third quarter of 2025, which reflects both our transition to commercialization of dual-use, multi-domain products and the near-term financial impacts of scaling a deep-tech, space-based enterprise."

Outlook

  • Management indicated that 2 additional LizzieSat spacecrafts are in production for a planned late 2026 launch, featuring "advanced software-defined imagers and increased onboard processing capability."
  • CEO Craig emphasized the expectation of "improved gross margins and stronger revenue visibility" as the mobile launcher 2 contract completes and more resources shift to "higher-margin satellite and data programs."
  • The company expects the Fortis VPX computing suite, which is "on track for year-end validation," to contribute revenue starting in 2026.

Financial Results

  • CFO Parekh reported total revenue for the first 9 months of 2025 was approximately $2.8 million, down from $3.8 million in the same period in 2024, attributing the decline to a strategic shift away from legacy contract work toward higher-value commercial space and AI-driven solutions.
  • For the 3 months ended September 30, 2025, total revenue reached $1.3 million, a decrease from about $1.9 million in Q3 2024.
  • Cost of revenue for the quarter rose to $2.6 million, up 42% from the prior year, primarily due to increased depreciation and higher input costs.
  • Gross profit for Q3 2025 was a loss of $1.3 million compared to a profit of $38,000 in Q3 2024, with CFO Parekh stating this was "primarily due to higher depreciation from recently capitalized assets, which are essential to future revenue streams, contract mix evolution, reduced contribution from legacy services as we transition to higher margin recurring revenue models."
  • SG&A expenses for the quarter totaled $4.3 million, up from $3.2 million in Q3 2024, reflecting "strategic head count growth aligned with our move to higher-value offerings, expanded mission operations for satellite support, increased software infrastructure investment, accrued equity compensation and employee bonuses and higher depreciation expense."
  • Adjusted EBITDA loss for Q3 2025 was $4.0 million, an increase from Q3 2024. Net loss for the quarter was $6 million compared to $3.9 million in the same period of the prior year.
  • As of September 30, 2025, Sidus had $12.7 million in cash.

Q&A

  • CEO Craig responded to a question about Fortis VPX commercialization, indicating the first three products are on track for release to production in January 2026 and that "customer integrations and revenue contributions" are expected soon after.
  • When asked about the LizzieSat-3 commissioning and the impact of additional satellites, Craig explained, "The LS-3 commissioning is progressing well because there are multiple payloads and sensors along with our integration of updated and enhanced software, it isn't a quick process. But our satellites have a 5-year design life, and we're manufactured with that time line in mind. The additional satellites in production, currently LS-4 and LS-5 expand hosted payload capability, data availability and on-orbit AI throughout."
  • On the Fortis VPX and FeatherEdge customer pipeline, Craig stated, "We have active early access programs with both government and commercial customers for our proven FeatherEdge platform. And several have already begun transitioning toward multiyear hardware agreements."
  • Regarding geographic revenue mix, Craig noted "strong momentum internationally, especially among allies seeking sovereign U.S. origin multi-domain capabilities."
  • On backlog, Craig indicated it is "increasingly being driven by VPX SOSA hardware, engineering services and LizzieSat integrations."
  • Discussing the recent capital raise, Craig said it was intended to "accelerate innovation and then fund growth."
  • On alignment with DoD priorities, Craig explained, "We believe Sidus' technology road map aligns directly with that need. Our strength in autonomy, rapid deployment, ruggedized edge computing and multi-mission sensing allow us to deliver space-based nodes that are capable of operating as part of a larger adaptive defense network."
  • Regarding the lunar-capable LizzieSat, Craig stated, "There are very few U.S. companies that could provide smaller, cost-effective lunar buses, and we believe our early-mover position creates a strategic opportunity."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' questions centered on product commercialization timelines, customer adoption, and revenue mix, with a neutral to slightly positive tone, seeking clarity on execution and market positioning.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing adaptability, recurring revenue, and innovation: "I'm confident that Sidus is built to remain resilient and adaptable regardless of the external landscape." Confidence was also reflected in statements about funding, technology progress, and alignment with defense priorities.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone remains largely neutral, while management's tone has grown more optimistic about commercialization, recurring revenue, and product launches.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter emphasized diversification, completion of the mobile launch 2 contract, and new capital raises, while the previous quarter focused more on the initial commercialization and the commissioning of LizzieSat-3.
  • There is a stronger emphasis this quarter on recurring revenue, defense and commercial customer expansion, and further product launches, particularly the late 2026 planned launches for LizzieSat-4 and LizzieSat-5.
  • Key metrics show revenue and profit declines compared to the previous year, with continued investment in infrastructure, personnel, and R&D.
  • Management's tone this quarter highlights increased confidence in the shift from R&D to commercialization and recurring revenue, while analysts' focus remains on execution and customer traction.
  • Strategic priorities have evolved from establishing product-market fit to accelerating commercialization and scaling production.

Risks and Concerns

  • CFO Parekh cited "ongoing global supply chain pressures impacting manufacturing operations" and higher input costs as challenges.
  • Management warned that "uncertainty around government funding and shifting federal budgets" could affect revenue timing but cited a diversified revenue model as a hedge.
  • The CFO also referenced "the near-term financial impacts of scaling a deep-tech, space-based enterprise."
  • Management indicated plans for "meaningful cost reduction activity and operating efficiencies to support long-term profitability."

Final Takeaway

Sidus Space management highlighted progress in vertical integration, rapid satellite deployment, and a strategic pivot toward recurring, diversified revenue streams. The company is preparing for the commercialization of new all-domain product lines and expects revenue contributions from the Fortis VPX suite beginning in 2026. With LizzieSat-4 and LizzieSat-5 in production for a targeted late 2026 launch and a completed capital raise supporting expansion, Sidus Space positions itself for scalable growth in commercial, defense, and civil markets, despite ongoing supply chain and budgetary uncertainties.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Modiv Industrial signals systematic asset recycling and heightened selectivity amid volatile deal market
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Modiv Industrial, Inc. (MDV) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Aaron Halfacre, President, CEO & Director, did not provide formal prepared remarks, instead stating the company had intentionally delayed its call to observe broader market reactions and noted, "I feel generally optimistic. I mean, look, I don't -- no one knows where December -- will Powell be in December. Are they done or not? But I think we all probabilistically underwrite that there's going to be a new Fed regime come May. And so -- and that regime has a high propensity to be easing." Halfacre emphasized the company’s durability and stronger balance sheet, saying, "I feel like our balance sheet is stronger. I feel like our [indiscernible] was better. I feel -- I just -- I have much more clarity now than I did even a year ago. And so I think that leads to optimism."
  • Raymond Pacini, Executive VP, CFO, Secretary & Treasurer, reported, "Yes, there was a $300,000 fee that we obtained for terminating some easement rights that are marked for property, and that's it."

Outlook

  • Management indicated a deliberate and systematic approach to asset recycling, with Halfacre explaining, "You should expect that we will be deliberate and systematic about asset recycling. If you think back, right, so we did the asset recycling the GFPR, which is a large bulk." He also stated the company’s buy box has become much tighter: "We've gotten much more selective. But that said, it feels right now at least and it's usually odd because candidly, you don't tend to see a lot at year-end."
  • The company is monitoring capital markets for signs of easing and increased deal flow, with Halfacre noting, "If we start to see real easing and we start to see some consistent trends for REITs... it's pretty cloudy. But even despite that cloudiness, I feel pretty optimistic about what I'm seeing."

Financial Results

  • A onetime $300,000 fee was recognized in other property income in the third quarter as disclosed by Pacini. There were no additional explicit financial metrics, such as revenue, AFFO, or EPS, stated in the transcript for the third quarter.
  • The company added another asset to the held-for-sale bucket, specifically the Clara property, with the aim to sell by year-end or early January. The Costco property remains held for sale, with KB Home expected to close by December 15, with the possibility of an extension to February 15.

Q&A

  • Craig Kucera, Lucid Capital Markets, LLC: Asked about onetime revenue adjustments and upcoming asset sales. Halfacre and Pacini responded by confirming the $300,000 fee was a onetime event and discussed the held-for-sale status of Clara and Costco properties.
  • Kucera: Inquired about acquisition opportunities. Halfacre observed, "We've probably looked at 4 or 5 deals in the last week," but emphasized increased selectivity and a healthier pipeline in terms of quantity, though quality remains a challenge.
  • Gaurav Mehta, Alliance Global Partners: Asked about cap rates and asset recycling's role in funding acquisitions. Halfacre reported cap rates mainly in the "7 handles" for target properties and reiterated a systematic approach to asset recycling.
  • John Massocca, B. Riley Securities: Questioned sources of future growth capital and impact of market conditions. Halfacre suggested the company is waiting for greater market clarity before accessing external growth capital, and prefers not to issue new equity or preferred unless market conditions improve.
  • Massocca: Sought insight on tenant sentiment regarding tariffs. Halfacre stated, "There's no blood in the streets and our businesses are operating. I mean most of our businesses buy U.S. and sell U.S., right?"
  • Stephen Chick: Asked about same-store rental income and property expense impacts from divestitures. Pacini said, "our overall average is 2.5% rent growth for the year just based on escalations in the leases."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious interest in deal flow, asset sales, and capital sources, with a neutral to slightly positive tone as they probed for clarity on transaction timing and capital recycling plans.
  • Management maintained an optimistic, candid, and somewhat gritty tone throughout, with Halfacre stating, "I feel pretty optimistic about what I'm seeing," but also acknowledging market volatility and the need for patience.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted from frustration over inactivity to cautious optimism about recent increases in deal opportunities and a clearer strategic direction.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company has moved from a previously "boring" or stagnant quarter to a more active approach, reporting increased deal flow and heightened selectivity.
  • Strategic focus has shifted toward systematic asset recycling, with more emphasis on timing and quality of acquisitions.
  • Analysts continued to focus on asset sales, acquisition opportunities, and capital market access, but now also pressed for specifics on timing and quality of pipeline deals.
  • Management's tone has become more optimistic, with greater clarity and confidence in the durability of the balance sheet and the company’s ability to adapt.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified market volatility, the unpredictability of capital flows, and the cloudy outlook for REITs as ongoing challenges.
  • Asset recycling and transaction timing face potential headwinds from cap rate movements and holiday season slowdowns.
  • Analysts probed for risks related to tenant sentiment, tariff impacts, and the ability to complete expected property sales on schedule.

Final Takeaway

Modiv Industrial, Inc. signals a period of growing optimism and operational clarity, underscored by a disciplined, systematic approach to asset recycling and heightened selectivity in acquisitions. Management sees increased deal flow and expects continued deliberate asset rotation, while maintaining confidence in the company’s resilience and balance sheet strength despite a volatile market environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nelson Peltz's Trian Fund exits U-Haul and Allstate in Q3
data.description:
Trian Fund Management exited its stake in trucks, trailers, and storage rental firm U-Haul (UHAL) and insurer Allstate (ALL) in Q3 2025. 

The fund disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing for the three months ended September 30, 2025, published on Friday.

Founded in 2005 by billionaire investor Nelson Peltz, Trian is famed for activist campaigns against companies such as Procter & Gamble (PG) and Walt Disney (DIS). According to Forbes, Peltz's real-time net worth is $1.6B.

The fund in Q3 also marginally increased its stake in fast-food chain Wendy's (WEN) by 2.2K shares.

More on 13F filings

  • Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway adds Google stake, exits D.R. Horton, among other Q3 trades
  • Carl Icahn's top Q3 moves include new stake in EchoStar, exiting Bausch Health
  • Druckenmiller's Duquesne takes in Amazon, Google, Meta, exits Microsoft, Lilly in Q3 moves
  • Seth Klarman's Baupost takes stake in Union Pacific, exits ViaSat, among Q3 trades
  • Renaissance Technologies lowers stake in Palantir, takes new position in Tesla in Q3 moves

data.title: Oil rallies as major Ukraine drone strike highlights vulnerability of Russian crude flows
data.description:
Oil futures rebounded Friday, with WTI back at the $60/bbl level after a major Ukrainian drone and missile strike on a key Russian Black Sea port revived concerns about supply disruptions.

The attack on the Novorossiysk port, which handles oil cargoes from Russia as well as oil exports from Kazakhstan, was one of the biggest on Russian oil-exporting infrastructure in recent months and reportedly caused a temporarily suspension of oil exports - amounting to the equivalent to 2.2 million bbl/day, or 2% of global supply.

Ukraine said it separately struck an oil refinery in Russia's Saratov region and a fuel storage facility in nearby Engels overnight.

"The intensity of these attacks has increased; it's much more often. Eventually, they could hit something that causes lasting disruption," UBS commodity analyst Giovanni Staunovo said in a note.

Also, Iran seized an oil tanker en route to Singapore after it passed the Strait of Hormuz. in the first major escalation of the tensions in the Middle East region since the Israel-Iran war in June.

Still, WTI futures remain down 16% YTD on growing expectations of an oversupplied market, with U.S. crude inventories rising last week in a sign of weaker demand in the world's largest consumer, and OPEC estimating that global supply outpaced demand in Q3 and forecasting a significant supply surplus.

Crude price gains continue to be limited by the fundamental backdrop, Joseph Dahrieh of Tickmill said in a note, with groups including the IEA forecasting a significant supply surplus, adding that "the market could remain under pressure despite occasional bounces driven by geopolitical headlines."

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for December delivery closed +2.4% on Friday to $60.09/bbl, and front-month Brent December crude finished +2.2% to $64.39/bbl; for the week, Nymex and Brent ended up 0.6% and 1.2%, respectively.

U.S. natural gas (NG1:COM) fell Friday but settled well above the day's lows, with the front-month Nymex December contract -1.7% to $5.5660/MMBtu after the EIA reported inventories rose by a higher than forecast 45 Bcf last week to 3,960 Bcf, or 172 Bcf above the five-year average; for the week, natgas rose for the fourth consecutive week, up 5.8%.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE), finished +1.8% this week.

Top 10 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Spruce Power (SPRU) +90.8%, Orion Energy Systems (OESX) +39.9%, FTC Solar (FTCI) +26.1%, Baytex Energy (BTE) +25.4%, Braskem (BAK) +23%, Drilling Tools International (DTI) +20.8%, Smart Sand (SND) +19.6%, Nexa Resources (NEXA) +19%, Albemarle (ALB) +18.5%, Canadian Solar (CSIQ) +17.5%.

Top 10 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: ESS Tech (GWH) -37.8%, Nuscale Power (SMR) -26%, New Era Energy & Digital (NUAI) -25.2%, American Resources (AREC) -24.7%, Eos Energy Enterprises (EOSE) -24.4%, ProFrac Holding (ACDC) -24%, Sable Offshore (SOC) -21.5%, Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) -19.8%, American Battery Technology (ABAT) -18.7%, Bloom Energy (BE) -17.2%.

Source: Barchart.com

data.title: SoftBank's Q3 moves include new stakes in Intel and Klarna, exiting Oracle
data.description:
SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) took new stakes in Intel (INTC) and Klarna (KLAR) in Q3 2025, among other moves. 

The Japanese multinational investment company disclosed new buys in Intel (INTC), adding about 87M shares to its holdings, as well as 15.4M new shares in Klarna (KLAR), as per its 13F filing for the quarter.

Looking at exits, SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) shed its positions in Oracle (ORCL) and Cipher Mining (CIFR).

Additionally, the company reduced its stake in T-Mobile (TMUS) by 29.3% to 45.2M shares and in Lemonade (LMND) by 40.2% to 3.6M shares.

SoftBank's (OTCPK:SFTBY) filing comes just a few days after its earnings report for the six months ended September 30, in which it revealed that it had unloaded its entire stake in the world's largest company, Nvidia (NVDA), for $5.83B in October. Friday's 13F filing showed that, just before that sale, the Japanese giant held 32.1M NVDA shares.      

More on 13F filings:

  • Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway adds Google stake, exits D.R. Horton, among other Q3 trades
  • Tudor Investment adds Meta, Broadom, Cyberark, among top Q3 trades
  • Renaissance Technologies lowers stake in Palantir, takes new position in Tesla in Q3 moves
  • Soros Capital adds Vimeo, exits Titan America, among Q3 trades

data.title: Dragonfly Energy outlines $13M Q4 2025 revenue target following debt restructuring and OEM expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Dragonfly Energy Holdings Corp. (DFLI) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Denis Phares, CEO and Interim CFO, highlighted a return to year-over-year revenue growth with sales increasing 26% to $16.0 million and gross margin expanding by over 700 basis points to nearly 30%. He stated, "we successfully executed a comprehensive capital raising and debt restructuring that fundamentally reshaped our balance sheet and greatly improved our liquidity." Phares emphasized the importance of these actions: "Since July, we raised approximately $90 million in gross proceeds through 3 unstructured common equity offerings. Then in early November, we finalized a transformative restructuring of our term debt." He described the debt restructuring, including a $45 million prepayment, $25 million of debt converted into preferred equity, and $5 million forgiven, reducing total debt principal to $19 million with a lower interest rate and more flexible covenants.
  • CEO Phares indicated these changes send a "strong signal to current and potential customers about the company's stability and long-term financial health," allowing the company to "dedicate more time and resources to business growth."
  • Wade Seaburg, Chief Commercial Officer, discussed momentum in the OEM business, noting expanded partnerships in the RV market with Airstream, Awaken RV, and Ember RV. Seaburg reported, "Battle Born Batteries are now standard across Airstream's 2026 motorized models," and outlined new partnerships: "Awaken selected Battle Born Batteries as the standard lithium power solution across their entire debut lineup..." and "we expanded our long-standing partnership with Ember RV making Battle Born Batteries standard across its 2026 Overland Series."
  • Seaburg highlighted growth in heavy-duty trucking, referencing production orders from a nationally recognized fleet after pilot programs and a collaboration with PACCAR, including a jointly authored Whitepaper. He commented, "We believe this collaboration provides credible third-party validation of our technology under demanding conditions and it has increased our visibility with large fleet operators."
  • CEO Phares announced two new U.S. patents, one strengthening the Dragonfly IntelLigence platform and the other for Wakespeed charge control technology. He also noted, "we received recognition of our domestic manufacturing capabilities through a $300,000 grant from the Nevada Tech Hub."

Outlook

  • Phares stated, "we expect net sales of approximately $13 million" for Q4 2025, projecting about 7% year-over-year growth in the "seasonably slowest quarter." He added, "We are forecasting adjusted EBITDA of approximately negative $3.3 million."
  • Management acknowledged missing the initial target of adjusted EBITDA breakeven by year-end, citing a volatile tariff environment, extended freight recession, macroeconomic uncertainty, and government shutdown impacts on industrial customers.
  • Phares expressed, "We remain confident in our ability to achieve profitability as we continue executing on our growth initiatives."

Financial Results

  • Net sales for Q3 2025 were $16 million, reflecting a 44% increase in OEM net sales. DTC customer sales totaled $5 million compared to $5.2 million previously.
  • Gross profit increased 65% to $4.7 million, with gross margin at 29.7%. Operating expenses decreased to $8.5 million from $8.9 million.
  • Net loss was $11.1 million versus a net loss of $6.8 million. Net loss per share was $0.20 compared to $0.98 per share. Adjusted EBITDA improved to negative $2.1 million from negative $5.5 million.

Q&A

  • George Gianarikas, Canaccord Genuity: Asked about Q4 guidance and which segment drives the sequential revenue change. CEO Phares responded that Q4 is "always the slowest quarter by seasonality" for OEM and that DTC segment visibility is low due to macroeconomic conditions and the government shutdown, leading to cautious guidance.
  • Gianarikas: Asked if normalization in consumer trends could lead to significant 2026 growth and about primary growth drivers. Phares said, "we're pretty confident about 2026... the trucking business that we're starting to break into right now is going to be the primary growth driver."
  • Gianarikas: Sought details on cash and share count post-transactions. Phares answered, "about 121 million shares, and a pro forma cash balance after the debt paydowns and everything is on the order of $30 million."
  • Gianarikas: Asked about growth initiatives enabled by the improved balance sheet. Phares indicated increased investment in sales manpower and product development, particularly in oil and gas and trucking markets.
  • Alfred Moore, ROTH Capital Partners: Inquired about early feedback from customers after the debt restructuring. Phares replied, "it was like a flip of the switch really. I mean, we're starting to get POs now... the conversations have completely changed over now to how do we get going with these projects."
  • Moore: Asked about EBITDA breakeven and other growth areas. Phares acknowledged the need for more volume but said, "the stage is set because we are getting better gross margins."
  • Moore: Queried government shutdown impacts. Phares said it is "a little early to see" effects but noted some important customers were unable to follow through with projects.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on revenue growth drivers, the improved balance sheet, and future opportunities, but maintained skepticism due to macro uncertainty and segment headwinds.
  • Management’s tone was confident and assertive during prepared remarks, emphasizing transformative changes and business momentum, but became more cautious in Q&A when addressing guidance, volume needs, and macro risks. Phares used phrases like "we believe" and "we are confident" while also acknowledging, "we need more volume to get back to where we anticipated."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence has increased regarding strategic positioning and balance sheet health, while analysts remain attentive to execution and visibility.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q4 2025 is more cautious, reflecting macroeconomic headwinds, a shift from the previous quarter’s expectation of sequential improvement and approaching EBITDA breakeven.
  • Strategic focus shifted from incremental operational improvements and pilot expansions (Q2) to a transformative balance sheet overhaul and aggressive OEM expansion (Q3).
  • Management tone evolved from steady optimism to assertive confidence following the debt restructuring, while analyst questions in both quarters consistently probed for growth visibility and execution in new markets.
  • Key metrics: Gross margin expanded further in Q3, but net loss increased compared to Q2. OEM growth accelerated, while DTC remained under macro pressure.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited macroeconomic uncertainty, volatile tariff environment, the extended freight recession, and the impact of the government shutdown on industrial customers as significant challenges.
  • Phares noted low visibility in the DTC segment and customer project delays tied to government funding disruptions.
  • Management’s mitigation includes a focus on OEM expansion, cost discipline, and leveraging the strengthened balance sheet to invest in growth areas.

Final Takeaway

Dragonfly Energy’s third quarter of 2025 marked a pivotal period, as the company executed a major balance sheet transformation, expanded OEM partnerships, and delivered substantial margin improvements. Management underscored the strengthened capital structure as a catalyst for pursuing growth initiatives in trucking and adjacent markets, while cautioning that macroeconomic headwinds and seasonality will temper near-term results. The company projects $13 million in Q4 2025 net sales and remains focused on building momentum toward profitability in 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Bit Digital outlines disciplined ETH accumulation and targets 1.2 exahash mining by mid-2026 while expanding staking operations
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bit Digital, Inc. (BTBT) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Samir Tabar stated that "the third quarter was our first full period as a focused Ethereum treasury and staking company." He highlighted that Bit Digital is prioritizing disciplined capital allocation and risk management as the company transitions to an ETH-centric platform, with a goal to "compound value per share through disciplined capital allocation, careful risk management and consistent yield generation."
  • Tabar reported significant growth in ETH holdings, noting "At quarter end, we held about 122,000 ETH. By the end of October, that number has risen to more than 153,000 ETH with roughly 132,000 actively stated. That is a 5-fold increase since June."
  • He announced the completion of a $150 million convertible notes offering after quarter end, with proceeds used to purchase about 31,000 ETH. The offering was "designed to be accretive to net asset value per share."
  • Tabar emphasized the increasing contribution of staking to revenue, stating, "Staking revenue grew to about $2.9 million in the third quarter, up from $400,000 in the prior quarter." He added, "as our ETH position grows, staking income will become the main engine of our results."
  • On mining, Tabar said, "We produced 65 Bitcoin in the third quarter down from 83% in the prior quarter as we continue to wind down the business in a measured way." Mining gross margin was 32%. He outlined that mining will continue to offset corporate overhead during the transition to a fully Ethereum-based model.
  • CFO Erke Huang reported, "Total revenue for the third quarter was $30.5 million compared to $25.7 million in the prior quarter and $22.8 million in the same period last year." Huang also stated, "Ethereum staking revenue totaled $2.9 million, up over 542% from last year."
  • Huang highlighted, "Net income for the third quarter was $146.7 million or $0.47 per diluted share compared to a net loss of $38.8 million in the year ago period. Results were driven by higher revenue, improved margins and $168 million gain on digital assets, reflecting appreciation in our Ethereum holdings."

Outlook

  • Tabar affirmed, "We will allocate capital responsibly. We will continue scaling our staking operations, and we will maintain a strong financial position." He added, "We anticipate active cash rate trending towards 1.2 exahash by mid-2026."
  • Management expects staking income to become the company's principal revenue source as ETH holdings and staking activities expand. Huang indicated, "We expect the full effect of this increase to be reflected in fourth quarter results."
  • The company reiterated a commitment to keeping total leverage below 20% of ETH holdings, with no plans to increase leverage until ETH price rises relative to convertible notes.

Financial Results

  • Third quarter total revenue was $30.5 million, with Ethereum staking revenue at $2.9 million. Digital asset mining revenue reached $7.4 million.
  • Gross profit was $18.3 million, representing a 60% gross margin. General and administrative expenses were $33.1 million, mainly due to "higher share-based compensation and consulting costs related to the WhiteFiber IPO and transition."
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $166.8 million. Cash and cash equivalents at quarter end totaled approximately $179 million, with $24 million in digital assets. Total liquidity, including USDC, was around $620 million.
  • No debt outstanding as of September 30, but a $150 million convertible notes offering closed after quarter end.

Q&A

  • George Sutton, Craig-Hallum: Asked about Bit Digital's belief in Ethereum versus other blockchain options. Tabar responded, "Ethereum has no downtime. And Wall Street is going to back a blockchain that has 0 downtime… It's pretty clear that Wall Street has already made its decision about which blockchain is going to that given those reasons that I mentioned."
  • George Sutton: Asked about the percentage of ETH staked. Huang replied, "We can take the 100% and right now, the reason were about like 85%, it's below 90% is because a portion of that we're working with external managers also being stated and by different like staking strategies that will generate alpha for the company as well."
  • Brian Dobson, Clear Street: Asked what could set Bit Digital apart from competition. Tabar explained, "Bit Digital was not…some sort of failed business that was a shell…This company currently still has a very profitable business, including staking Ethereum on the balance sheet."
  • Kevin Dede, H.C. Wainwright: Inquired about mining hash rate outlook and validator node operation. William Schnier responded, "It's just a function of sort of a hosting portfolio pruning over time as contracts roll off and then optimizing the newer machines…it's going to be a business that methodically winds down." Huang added that Bit Digital "work[s] with FitMint for our native staking, and we have been very happy with the service and security as well."
  • Henry Hearle, B. Riley (for Nick Giles): Asked about consolidation and M&A. Tabar said, "We're uniquely positioned, and there's just no space I'd rather be in the digital assets and artificial intelligence…If we were to buy another debt, I'm unsure they'd add value really. I think we'll just continue to stay the course and buy Ethereum."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised questions about competitive differentiation, staking strategy, capital allocation, and operational efficiency, with a generally inquisitive but neutral tone.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently referencing discipline, prudence, and strategic advantage. Tabar repeatedly emphasized, "discipline is more than a strategy is who we are."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remains confident but is now more focused on demonstrating tangible results from the Ethereum strategy and WhiteFiber IPO. Analysts remain persistent but less skeptical than before.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company transitioned from initiating its Ethereum strategy in Q2 to full execution in Q3, as reflected by the fivefold increase in ETH holdings.
  • Staking revenue increased from $0.4 million in Q2 to $2.9 million in Q3, and total revenue grew to $30.5 million from $25.7 million. Net income swung from $14.9 million in Q2 to $146.7 million in Q3, largely due to the digital asset gain.
  • Guidance shifted from a focus on building ETH holdings and unwinding mining, to active deployment of convertible note proceeds and scaling staking operations.
  • Analysts in both quarters probed for clarity on operational transitions and capital allocation, but the Q3 focus shifted toward differentiation, M&A, and staking strategies.
  • Management demonstrated increased confidence in execution and the durability of its ETH-centric model.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the ongoing wind-down of the Bitcoin mining business as a risk area but highlighted that it remains a positive margin contributor during the transition.
  • Elevated G&A expenses were acknowledged as being driven by one-off items related to the WhiteFiber IPO and transition, with expectations for a normalized, leaner cost base moving forward.
  • Tabar pointed to the complexity and duration of WhiteFiber contract negotiations as a challenge, noting "there are a lot of moving parts when it comes to a contract that is generationally long and that has this kind of quantum amount to it."
  • Huang stated that leverage will not increase until "the ETH price rises to a comfortable level relative to our notes."

Final Takeaway

Bit Digital’s third quarter marked a decisive shift to an Ethereum-focused treasury and staking model, with a sharp increase in ETH holdings, robust revenue from staking, and a substantial gain in net income attributed to digital asset appreciation. The company completed a $150 million convertible note offering to further expand its ETH position, while maintaining a disciplined approach to capital allocation and risk management. Mining operations continue to wind down, serving as a bridge during the transition. Management projects ongoing growth in staking income and emphasizes a focus on long-term value creation through prudent execution and strategic asset positioning, particularly in Ethereum and AI infrastructure via WhiteFiber.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Corvex Management adds Disney, trims Dollar Tree position, and exits Alphabet in Q3 moves
data.description:
Corvex Management took new positions in Salesforce (CRM), Union Pacific (UNP) and The Walt Disney (DIS), trimmed its positions in Dollar Tree (DLTR) and MGM Resorts International (MGM), while exiting Alphabet (GOOGL) (GOOG) and Sempra (SRE) among its Q3 moves.

The fund disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing, which was published on Friday.

The Keith Meister-led fund acquired 1,308,000 shares in Disney, 8,600,275 shares in Cemex (CX), 1,290,810 shares in Carnival (CCL), 677,863 shares in Union Pacific, and 14,500 shares in Salesforce.

According to the filing, the fund increased its position in Oracle (ORCL) from 99,500 shares to 349,500 shares; in Amazon (AMZN) from 675,029 shares to 977,529 shares; in Restaurant Brands International (QSR) from 873,182 shares to 2,408,743 shares, and in IAC (IAC) from 2,000,920 shares to 2,788,379 shares.

On the other hand, the fund reduced its position in Dollar Tree from 981,899 shares to 541,300 shares and in MGM Resorts International from 5,627,478 shares to 5,385,478 shares.

The fund exited its position in Alphabet, Sempra, Norfolk Southern (NSC), CSX Corporation (CSX) and Integral Ad Science (IAS).

data.title: Third Point's Q3 moves include new stakes in Norfolk, Union Pacific amid their $85B merger
data.description:
Billionaire investor Daniel Loeb's Third Point made several moves in Q3 2025, including taking new stakes in Norfolk Southern (NSC) and Union Pacific (UNP) amid the two railroad companies' mega $85B merger attempt.

The fund disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing for the three months ended September 30, 2025, published on Friday.

Third Point picked up 875K shares in Union Pacific (UNP) and 1.65M shares in Norfolk (NSC) in the quarter. Back in July, UNP said it would acquire NSC for $320/share, valuing it at $85B. Shareholders of both companies approved the transaction on Friday. 

Looking at exits in the quarter, Loeb's fund unloaded its positions in exchange operator Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), enterprise management cloud software provider Workday (WDAY), and industrial pumps and valves maker Flowserve (FLS).

Turning to notable stake increases, Third Point nearly tripled its position in tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) to 1.1M shares in Q3. It also slightly increased its position in the world's largest company, Nvidia (NVDA), by 1.8% to 2.85M shares.

Finally, looking at stake decreases in the quarter, the fund trimmed asset manager Apollo (APO) by 49% to 650K shares and U.S. bank Capital One Financial (COF) by 23.2% to 1.385M shares.

Founded by Loeb in 1995, Third Point became famous for activist campaigns against companies such as Intel (INTC) and Campbell's (CPB). According to Forbes, Loeb's real-time net worth is $3.8B. 

More on 13F filings

  • Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway adds Google stake, exits D.R. Horton, among other Q3 trades
  • Carl Icahn's top Q3 moves include new stake in EchoStar, exiting Bausch Health
  • Druckenmiller's Duquesne takes in Amazon, Google, Meta, exits Microsoft, Lilly in Q3 moves
  • Seth Klarman's Baupost takes stake in Union Pacific, exits ViaSat, among Q3 trades
  • Renaissance Technologies lowers stake in Palantir, takes new position in Tesla in Q3 moves

data.title: Dundee Preference shares Series 3 GAAP EPS of $4.69
data.description:
• Dundee Preference shares Series 3 press release (DC.A:CA): Q3 GAAP EPS of $4.69.

  • In the third quarter of 2025, the Corporation reported net earnings before taxes from the mining investments segment of $91.4 million (2024 – $10.4 million). Performance from the mining portfolio investments generated income of $81.8 million (2024 – $9.0 million). The share of income from equity accounted mining investments during the third quarter of 2025 was $9.0 million (2024 – $0.7 million). Drivers of performance are described in the highlights above. During the same period, the Corporation reported net income from its royalty interest in the Borborema Gold Project of $430,000, which included $668,000 of royalty revenue.
